agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"OrderFulfillmentWorker","to":"RabbitMQ (order.fulfillment queue)","status":"risk","issue":"RabbitMQ queues order.fulfillment and order.fulfillment.dlq must be provisioned before deployment. Infrastructure team has not been notified — queues do not exist yet."},{"from":"OrderFulfillmentWorker","to":"RabbitMQ (order.fulfillment.dlq)","status":"risk","issue":"Dead-letter queue routing requires broker-side configuration (x-dead-letter-exchange, x-dead-letter-routing-key). Unknown if this is handled by the worker or expected to be pre-configured."},{"from":"Upstream producer","to":"RabbitMQ (order.fulfillment queue)","status":"risk","issue":"No producer identified. Something must publish messages to order.fulfillment — unclear which service produces orders and whether its message schema matches the worker's expected contract."}],"contract_compliance":"The original spec said 'process orders asynchronously' but did not specify RabbitMQ, queue names, or new environment variables. The implementation introduced infrastructure dependencies beyond the spec scope without stakeholder sign-off.","data_flow_integrity":"Cannot verify end-to-end data flow without knowing the upstream producer, message schema, and whether the DLQ is configured at the broker level. Risk of silent message loss if queue bindings are misconfigured."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["Verify structured logs include correlation/order IDs for tracing individual order processing","Confirm DLQ publish failures are logged — if logging and DLQ both fail, messages vanish silently"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"p99 latency exported — good baseline","gaps":["No mention of queue depth metrics (consumer lag)","No DLQ depth metric — critical for alerting on poison messages","No message throughput (processed/sec) metric","No error rate / failure rate metric reported"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No mention of distributed tracing (trace ID propagation from producer through worker)","Cannot correlate an order placed in the API to its async fulfillment without trace context"]},"debugging_capability":"Structured logging is a solid foundation. However, without trace ID propagation and queue depth metrics, debugging a backlog or stuck consumer at 3am will require manually inspecting RabbitMQ management UI."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"RabbitMQ becomes unreachable","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Implement reconnection with exponential backoff. Ensure /health endpoint reflects broker connectivity status."},{"scenario":"Poison message causes repeated processing failure","handling":"partial","recommendation":"DLQ exists which is good. Verify max retry count before DLQ routing. Without it, a poison message could loop indefinitely before hitting DLQ."},{"scenario":"DLQ publish itself fails","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add fallback logging of the failed message payload so it can be recovered manually. Alert on DLQ publish failures."},{"scenario":"Consumer falls behind (queue depth grows)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add queue depth metric and alert threshold. Consider autoscaling consumers or at minimum documenting manual scaling runbook."},{"scenario":"Worker crashes mid-processing","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Verify messages use manual ack (not auto-ack). If auto-ack, a crash means message loss. Manual ack with redelivery is required for at-least-once semantics."}],"retry_config":"Not specified. Must define max retries before DLQ routing to prevent infinite redelivery loops.","timeout_config":"Not specified. Message processing timeout should be set to prevent a single slow order from blocking the consumer.","circuit_breakers":"Not present. If fulfillment depends on downstream services (payment, inventory), a circuit breaker prevents cascade failure.","graceful_degradation":"Not described. Should define behavior when RabbitMQ is down — do upstream services queue locally, return errors, or drop requests?"},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":false,"rollback_plan":"Rollback is risky: if the queue has accumulated messages, rolling back the worker leaves unprocessed messages. Need a plan for draining or preserving queue state during rollback. The new queues themselves cannot be 'rolled back' without broker admin intervention.","monitoring_coverage":"Partial. Logging and p99 latency are present, but missing queue depth, error rate, DLQ depth, and consumer lag metrics — all critical for production operability.","on_call_documentation":"No runbook mentioned. On-call engineer needs: how to check queue depth, how to replay DLQ messages, how to scale consumers, how to drain the queue for maintenance.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":["RABBITMQ_URL","FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME"],"new_services":["RabbitMQ broker (or confirm existing broker)"],"new_queues_topics":["order.fulfillment","order.fulfillment.dlq"],"config_changes":["DLQ routing configuration at broker level (dead-letter-exchange, dead-letter-routing-key)","Max retry / redelivery policy"],"infra_provisioning":["RabbitMQ queue provisioning (both queues)","Secrets management for RABBITMQ_URL","Network/firewall rules if broker is on separate infrastructure"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No database migrations mentioned. Data safety concern is message loss in queues if deployment or rollback is mishandled."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"RabbitMQ queues not provisioned and infrastructure team not notified","reason":"The implementation requires two new RabbitMQ queues (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) plus broker-level DLQ routing config plus two new environment variables (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME). None of this was in the original spec and infrastructure team is unaware. Deploying without these queues will cause immediate startup failure.","routed_to":"Coach K → Infrastructure team"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"New environment variables require secrets management provisioning","reason":"RABBITMQ_URL likely contains credentials. Must be provisioned in secrets manager and injected into the deployment environment before release. FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME must also be set in all target environments.","routed_to":"Coach K → Infrastructure team"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No queue depth, DLQ depth, or consumer lag metrics","reason":"Without queue depth monitoring, a growing backlog is invisible until orders are noticeably delayed. Without DLQ depth alerting, poison messages accumulate silently. On-call cannot detect or diagnose queue-related issues.","routed_to":"Coach K → Shaq (implementation gap)"},{"type":"integration_risk","issue":"No upstream producer identified or contract defined","reason":"The worker consumes from order.fulfillment but no producer is identified. Without a defined message schema contract, the first integration will likely fail on schema mismatch. This is a cross-team integration risk.","routed_to":"Coach K → MJ (architecture gap)"}],"confidence":{"level":82,"high_confidence_areas":["Infrastructure gap assessment — queues clearly not provisioned","Environment variable requirements are concrete and verifiable","DLQ configuration is a well-known operational requirement"],"low_confidence_areas":["Actual reconnection/retry behavior — would need to read implementation code","Whether auto-ack or manual-ack is used — critical for message safety","Downstream dependencies of the fulfillment process itself"],"assumptions":["No existing RabbitMQ broker is pre-configured with these queues","RABBITMQ_URL contains connection credentials","The worker uses a standard AMQP client library","No IaC (Terraform, etc.) was updated alongside this implementation"]}}
